publisherName: City of Joliet Department of Public Utilities
claimSummary: Joliet says its interactive map shows water service material by address, prioritizes pre-1940s homes for inventory work, and offers free inspections when the private side is still unknown.
reviewerNote: Official Joliet lead service line information page reviewed manually for map coverage and inspection language.

publisherName: City of Joliet Department of Public Utilities
claimSummary: Joliet says qualifying homes can receive free water testing and that point-of-use pitchers are provided at no cost when lead lines elevated tests or disturbance risks trigger interim protection guidance.
reviewerNote: Official Joliet notice and customer guidance reviewed manually for free testing and interim pitcher support.

publisherName: City of Joliet Department of Public Utilities
claimSummary: Joliet says it proactively replaces lead water service lines and covers full replacement at no cost when a leak repair elevated water test or water main rehabilitation project triggers the program.
reviewerNote: Official Joliet replacement program language reviewed manually for trigger-based no-cost replacement rules.

publisherName: City of Joliet Department of Public Utilities
claimSummary: Joliet says full lead service line replacement is no cost to the homeowner when specific program triggers are met and that affected homes can also receive point-of-use pitchers at no cost.
reviewerNote: Official Joliet cost coverage language reviewed manually for trigger-based no-cost replacement and pitcher support.

claimSummary: Rockford says customers can use its interactive map to identify public and private service line materials and can submit a digital survey to improve private-side material records.
reviewerNote: Official Rockford lead page reviewed manually for inventory details.

claimSummary: Rockford says free annual lead testing is available, explains how lead can enter water through service lines and plumbing, and publishes exposure-reduction guidance on the same page.
reviewerNote: Official Rockford notice and testing guidance reviewed manually for testing and exposure-reduction language.

claimSummary: Rockford says construction has commenced on a citywide lead service line replacement project financed through the Illinois SRF and that owners can also coordinate voluntary full replacements with the Water Division when they pay for the private-side segment.
reviewerNote: Official Rockford replacement program page reviewed manually for SRF project and voluntary replacement rules.

publisherName: City of Evanston Water Production Bureau
claimSummary: Evanston says its public water service information map lets residents check an address or billing account for possible lead service exposure and that the city submitted a draft replacement plan in April 2025.
reviewerNote: Official Evanston lead replacement site reviewed manually for map coverage and draft-plan timing.

publisherName: City of Evanston Water Production Bureau
claimSummary: Evanston says no-additional-cost replacement is already active in annual project areas and that the city updates its service line inventory and disturbance notifications each year under Illinois requirements.
reviewerNote: Official Evanston lead replacement site reviewed manually for project-area and annual notification language.

publisherName: City of Evanston Water Production Bureau
claimSummary: Evanston says its homeowner-initiated program coordinates simultaneous public and private replacement, uses licensed plumbers plus city inspection, and offers a $2500 reimbursement with waived permit fees.
reviewerNote: Official Evanston homeowner-initiated program page reviewed manually for reimbursement and permit language.

publisherName: City of Evanston Water Production Bureau
claimSummary: Evanston says annual project blocks receive full replacement at no additional cost to the homeowner and that homeowner-initiated private-side work may qualify for a $2500 reimbursement plus waived permit fees when owners use the pilot path.
reviewerNote: Official Evanston cost and reimbursement language reviewed manually for project-area versus homeowner-initiated paths.
